checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=1007476810.58 (2010-05-30) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=922166274.2 (2009-05-31); SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14156132497.1 (FY2010) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14078946773.71 (FY2009)
ReceivablesNetCurrent=1124222635.31 (2011-05-29) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=1007476810.58 (2010-05-30); SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14392719313.41 (FY2011) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14156132497.1 (FY2010)
ReceivablesNetCurrent=1399015417.46 (2013-05-26) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=1280238389.49 (2012-05-27); SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17191814111.94 (FY2013) vs SalesRevenueGoodsNet=16112181224.1 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=6444717731.3 (2009-05-31) growing to Goodwill=8454835154.26 (2016-05-29), a ~31% increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14078946773.71 (FY2009) to SalesRevenueGoodsNet=16020486906.09 (FY2016), a ~14% increase

IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill jumped from 3688374924.92 (2011-05-29) to 4774101422.5 (2011-08-28) in a single quarter, coincident with Goodwill rising from 6529641371.82 (2011-05-29) to 8029443159.93 (2011-08-28)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=1556578755.06 (2011-05-29) vs InventoryNet=1299970078.17 (2010-05-30), a ~19.7% increase, while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only ~1.67% (14392719313.41 FY2011 vs 14156132497.1 FY2010)

CostOfGoodsSold=11298422976.3 (FY2015) / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17052725051.49 (FY2015) implies gross margin ~33.7%, down from CostOfGoodsSold=8545949128.49 (FY2010)/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=14156132497.1 (FY2010) implying ~39.6% gross margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss margin fluctuates from NetIncomeLoss=1794422982.91/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17191814111.94 (~10.4%, FY2013) down to NetIncomeLoss=1181289774.16/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=17052725051.49 (~6.9%, FY2015) then back up to NetIncomeLoss=1641792567.48/SalesRevenueGoodsNet=16020486906.09 (~10.3%, FY2016)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=15282386.34 (2010-05-30) rising to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=28630293.39 (2016-05-29), while ReceivablesNetCurrent grew from 1007476810.58 (2010-05-30) to 1316219704.15 (2016-05-29) - allowance as % of receivables rose from ~1.52% to ~2.18%

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2007-01-08 and 10-Q/A filing_date=2007-01-08 filed on the same date, suggesting a restatement of prior period financials
NT 11-K filing_date=2003-11-26 indicating a late filing notification for an employee benefit plan report

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: LongTermDebt series ends at LongTermDebt=6716415346.6 (2012-02-26) with no further instant values provided in later periods, limiting debt-trend analysis through 2017

NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities quarterly detail is only provided for Q1 of each fiscal year after FY2014 (e.g., start 2015-06-01 end 2015-08-30 value=416783561.52), with no Q2-Q4 quarterly cash flow figures disclosed in the dataset for FY2015-FY2017

top_signals: Receivables grew faster than revenue in several consecutive fiscal years (FY2010, FY2011, FY2013), e.g. ReceivablesNetCurrent=1124222635.31 (2011-05-29) vs prior year 1007476810.58, outpacing SalesRevenueGoodsNet growth of only ~1.67%

Goodwill and intangible assets grew markedly faster than revenue (Goodwill +31% vs revenue +14% from FY2009 to FY2016), largely from a discrete acquisition-driven jump in August 2011 (Goodwill=8029443159.93 at 2011-08-28 vs 6529641371.82 at 2011-05-29)

Inventory spiked ~19.7% in FY2011 while revenue grew only ~1.67%, and gross margin compressed from ~39.6% (FY2010) to ~33.7% (FY2015), suggesting possible inventory build/markdown risk, though operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income (a reassuring quality-of-earnings signal) and allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of receivables actually increased over time rather than declining
